task: Create a new course
content_text: Create a course from scratch using the two-step Create Course dialog. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar. 2. On the Explore Courses page, click 'Create Course' (top right). 3. In Step 1 (Overview), enter the course name (required); fill any optional fields shown such as description, tags, learning outcomes, preview image, banner, or media. 4. Click 'Next'. 5. In Step 2 (Structure), choose the course structure depth and add your Levels and Sessions. 6. Click 'Create' to finish. The new course opens in the course list.

task: Create a course with AI
content_text: Generate a course outline and slides with the AI Course Creator instead of building it manually. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar. 2. On the Explore Courses page, click 'Create Course with AI' (if your role has it enabled). 3. You are taken to the AI Copilot, where you describe the course and let it generate the outline. Note: the 'Create Course with AI' button only appears when enabled in your role's display settings.

task: Choose the course structure (levels of depth)
content_text: Pick how many content levels your course uses while creating it. 1. Start creating a course and reach Step 2 (Structure). 2. Select a structure type: 2-Level (Chapter > Slide), 3-Level (Module > Chapter > Slide), 4-Level (Subject > Module > Chapter > Slide), or 5-Level (adds an extra grouping). 3. The selected depth determines whether you add Subjects, Modules, and Chapters inside the course. 4. Continue setting up Levels and Sessions, then click 'Create'. If your institute fixes the course depth, this selector is hidden and the default is used.

task: Add a level to a course
content_text: Add a level (e.g. Basic, Year 1) to an existing course. 1. Open the Courses section and click a course card to open its details. 2. Use the 'Add Level' option on the course details page. 3. Enter the level name (required) and the Duration in days. 4. Select the academic session(s) the level belongs to, or add a new session inline. 5. Submit to save the level.

task: Add a subject to a course
content_text: Add a subject inside a course that uses a subject-level structure. 1. Open the Courses section and click a course to open its details. 2. Navigate into the level/session whose content you want to edit. 3. Click 'Add Subject'. 4. In the dialog, enter the subject name. 5. Save to add the subject; you can then add modules inside it.

task: Add a module and chapter
content_text: Add modules and chapters to organize a course's content. 1. Open a course and drill into a subject (or into the level directly for shorter structures). 2. Click 'Add Module' and enter the module name to create it. 3. Open the module, then click 'Add Chapter'. 4. Enter the chapter details and click 'Add Chapter' to save. Chapters hold the actual slides (videos, PDFs, quizzes, etc.).

task: Add a slide to a chapter
content_text: Add a content slide inside a chapter. 1. Open a course, drill down to a chapter, and open it to view its slides. 2. Click 'Add Slide' in the slides sidebar. 3. Choose a slide type from the dropdown: PDF Document, PPT Presentation, Document, Video, Question, Assignment, Presentation, Quiz, Audio, Code Editor, SCORM Package, or Assessment. 4. Complete the dialog for that type (upload a file, paste a link, or fill the form). 5. The new slide is added to the chapter.

task: Upload a video slide
content_text: Add a video to a chapter either as an uploaded file or a hosted link. 1. Open the chapter and click 'Add Slide'. 2. Hover 'Video' and choose 'Upload video file', 'YouTube video', or 'Vimeo video'. 3. For an uploaded file, select the video from your device in the 'Upload Video File' dialog. 4. For YouTube, paste a valid YouTube URL in the 'Add YouTube Video' dialog (the title is fetched automatically). 5. Confirm to add the video slide to the chapter.

task: Upload a PDF or PPT slide
content_text: Add a PDF or PowerPoint as a slide in a chapter. 1. Open the chapter and click 'Add Slide'. 2. Select 'PDF Document' to upload a PDF, or 'PPT Presentation' to upload a PPT/PPTX (it is converted to PDF). 3. In the upload dialog, choose the file from your device. 4. Wait for the upload to finish; the slide is added to the chapter. To add an editable text document instead, choose 'Document' > 'Create new document'.

task: Add a quiz or question slide
content_text: Add interactive assessment content to a chapter. 1. Open the chapter and click 'Add Slide'. 2. Choose 'Question' to create a single interactive question, 'Quiz' for a timed quiz slide, or 'Assessment' to link an existing assessment or create a new one in the slide. 3. Fill out the question/quiz content in the dialog or in-slide form. 4. Save to add it to the chapter.

task: Publish a slide to learners
content_text: Publish a slide so enrolled learners can see it. 1. Open the chapter and select the slide you want to publish. 2. Click 'Publish' to open the Publish Slide dialog. 3. Optionally enable the notify option to alert learners. 4. Confirm to publish the slide. Newly created or edited slides stay in draft/under-review status until published.

task: Bulk add multiple slides at once
content_text: Quickly add several slides in one pass instead of one at a time. 1. Open the chapter and view its slides. 2. Click the 'Quick Add' button (lightning icon) in the slides sidebar, or pick 'Quick Add (Bulk)' from the 'Add Slide' dropdown. 3. Use the Quick Add view to upload and add multiple slides in a single flow.

task: Find and filter the course catalogue
content_text: Browse and narrow down courses on the Explore Courses page. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar. 2. Use the search box ('Search courses...') and press Enter to search by name. 3. Open the Filters panel to filter by Level, Tag, Teacher, or Academic Session. 4. Toggle between list view and package view using the view switch near the sort control. 5. Use 'Sort by' to order results Oldest or Newest. Use the tabs (Authored Courses, All Courses) to switch which set you are viewing.

task: Make a course public in the catalogue vs private
content_text: Control whether a course appears in the public catalogue with the 'Publish to ... Catalogue' checkbox in the course's create/edit form. 1. Open the Courses section; each course card shows its visibility with a badge: 'In Catalog' (Eye icon = public) or 'Private' (eye-slash icon = not public). 2. Create a new course, or open an existing course and edit it, to reach Step 2 of the course form. 3. In the 'Course Catalogue' section, tick the 'Publish to <Course> Catalogue' checkbox to publish it to the catalogue, or leave it unchecked to keep it private. 4. Save the course. Once it is published, its card shows the 'In Catalog' badge; otherwise it shows 'Private'. (If your institute has auto-publish-to-catalogue turned on in Course Settings, this checkbox is hidden and courses are published automatically.)

task: Submit a course for review
content_text: Send a draft course for admin approval before it goes live. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar and go to the 'Authored Courses' tab. 2. On a course card that is still in DRAFT, click the green 'Submit for Review' button (paper-plane icon) next to 'View Course' (this button shows for non-admin authoring roles on draft courses). 3. The course moves to the '<Courses> In Review' tab, where you can track its status and click 'Withdraw from Review' to pull it back. Admins review and approve submissions from the '<Course> Approval' tab. (Note: if your role has direct-edit of published courses enabled, you edit in place instead of going through this approval path.)

task: Add a new learner
content_text: Add a brand-new learner while enrolling them into a course. 1. Open the Learners list from the sidebar (Manage Contacts > Linked Course Contacts) at /manage-students/students-list. 2. Click the 'Enroll' button in the top-right of the Learner Management header. 3. In Step 1 (Select Learners), open the 'Add Manually' tab. 4. Fill in the learner's details (Full Name, Email/Mobile, etc.) and add them to the selection list. 5. Click 'Next' and pick the course/batch in Step 2 (Select Courses). 6. Set options in Step 3 (Enrollment Config), then click 'Preview'. 7. Review Step 4 and click 'Confirm Enrollment'.

task: Bulk-import learners from a CSV file
content_text: Import many learners at once using a CSV file. 1. Open the Learners list at /manage-students/students-list and click 'Enroll'. 2. In Step 1 (Select Learners), open the 'Import CSV' tab. 3. Click 'Download Template' (optionally use 'Choose template columns' to include extra fields), then fill it in — Full Name plus Email or Mobile Number are required. 4. Upload the completed CSV by dragging it in or selecting the file. 5. Review the preview and fix any flagged errors, then add the rows. 6. Click 'Next' and choose the course/batch in Step 2. 7. Set options in Step 3, click 'Preview', then 'Confirm Enrollment' in Step 4.

task: Enroll a learner into a batch or course
content_text: Enroll existing or new learners into one or more courses/batches. 1. Open the Learners list at /manage-students/students-list and click the 'Enroll' button. 2. In Step 1, use 'Search Existing' to find current learners (or 'From Course' to pull learners from another course) and add them. 3. Click 'Next' to Step 2 (Select Courses) and pick the course, level/session and batch. 4. In Step 3 (Enrollment Config) set access days and notification options. 5. Click 'Preview' to see the summary. 6. Click 'Confirm Enrollment' in Step 4 to finish.

task: Invite learners with an invite link
content_text: Create a shareable link that lets learners enroll themselves into a course. 1. Open the Invite page from the sidebar (Manage Contacts > Invite Users) at /manage-students/invite. 2. Click 'Create Invite Link'. 3. In the dialog, choose a Course, then select a Session and Level and click 'Add Batch' (add more batches if needed). 4. Click 'Continue' to open the summary, configure the invite details, and generate the link. 5. Back on the Invite Link List, use the copy control next to each invite to copy and share the link.

task: Copy and share an existing invite link
content_text: Find and share an invite link you already created. 1. Open the Invite page at /manage-students/invite. 2. Use the search box ('Search by name') or the Course/Level/Session filters and click 'Apply Filters' to find the invite. 3. On the matching card in the Invite Link List, click the copy control next to 'Invite Link' to copy it. 4. Use the card's menu (three dots) to edit the invite, change its status, or view details before sharing.

task: Review and approve learner enrollment requests
content_text: Approve or decline learners who requested or were invited to enroll. 1. Open the Enroll Requests page at /manage-students/enroll-requests. 2. The list defaults to Invited and Pending-for-approval learners; use the search and filters to narrow it down. 3. Select one or more rows using the checkboxes. 4. Use the bulk actions to Accept (approve) or Decline the selected requests. 5. Confirm in the Accept Request / Decline Request dialog that appears.

task: View a learner's profile
content_text: Open a learner's full profile from the list. 1. Open the Learners list at /manage-students/students-list. 2. Search by name or use the filters to find the learner. 3. Click the learner's name/row to open the profile side panel. 4. Use the panel tabs — Overview, Courses, Progress, Tests, Payment History, Enrol/Deroll, Portal Access, Files, Reports and more — to see their details. 5. Use the row's three-dot menu for actions like Change Batch, Extend Session, Re-register or Delete.

task: See a learner's course progress
content_text: Check how far a learner has progressed through their courses. 1. Open the Learners list at /manage-students/students-list. 2. Find and click the learner's name/row to open their profile side panel. 3. Open the 'Courses' tab to see enrolled courses, or the 'Progress' tab for learning completion. 4. Open the 'Tests' tab to review their test/assessment records. 5. For institute-wide activity and engagement analytics, use Learner Insights at /learner-insights.

task: View learning reports
content_text: Open the Learning Reports section for learner performance and progress. 1. From the sidebar, click 'Learning Reports' (under the LMS section) to open /study-library/reports. 2. Use the tabs at the top of the Reports page to switch between report views. 3. Select the course/batch and learner as prompted to drill into a specific learner's report.

task: View learner activity insights
content_text: See engagement and activity analytics for your learners. 1. Open Learner Insights at /learner-insights. 2. Review the widgets — Real-Time Active Users, User Activity Summary, Daily Activity Trend, Hourly Activity, Currently Active Users and Most Active Users — for an overview of learner engagement across the institute.

task: Find a specific learner by name, batch, or course
content_text: Search and filter the learner list to find specific learners. 1. Open the Learners list at /manage-students/students-list. 2. Type in the search box and press Enter to search by name. 3. Add filter chips such as Batch, Course, Level, Session, Status or Gender. 4. Click 'Apply Filters' (or the filter button) to update the table. 5. Use 'Clear Filters' / 'Reset All' to start over. The Total/Active/Inactive counts at the top reflect the current view.

task: Create a new batch
content_text: A batch links a Course, Session, and Level together so learners can be enrolled. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches from the sidebar. 2. Click the 'Create Batch' button (top right). 3. Step 1 - Select Course: choose 'Select existing course' and pick one, or 'Create new course' and add it, then click 'Next Step'. 4. Step 2 - Select Session: choose an existing session or 'Create new session' (enter a name and start date), then 'Next Step'. 5. Step 3 - Select Level: pick an existing level or create a new one, optionally tick 'Duplicate study materials from a pre-existing session', then click 'Create Batch'. 6. A 'Batch created successfully' toast confirms it and the batch appears in the list.

task: Find and switch between batches by session
content_text: Batches are grouped by academic session on the Batches page. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches from the sidebar. 2. The 'Learner Batches' page loads with the active session selected by default. 3. Use the session dropdown at the top right ('Select Session') to switch to another session. 4. Batches are shown grouped under each course (package) name as cards. 5. If a session has no batches, you'll see a 'No batches found' message with a 'Create Batch' option.

task: Enroll learners into a batch
content_text: You can enroll learners directly from a batch card. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches and select the session containing the batch. 2. Find the batch card and click 'Enroll Learner'. 3. Complete the multi-step enrollment wizard (learner details, then later steps for batch/credentials). 4. Click through each step using the footer buttons and 'Finish' on the last step. 5. The learner is added to that batch; the card's learner count updates.

task: Share a batch invite link with learners
content_text: Every batch has an invite code learners can use to join. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches and select the session. 2. Find the batch card - the 'Invite Code' is shown below the learner count. 3. Click the copy icon next to the invite code to copy the full invite link to your clipboard. 4. Share that link with learners so they can self-enroll into the batch.

task: View the learners in a batch
content_text: Open a batch to see its enrolled learners. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches and select the session. 2. On the batch card, click 'View Batch'. 3. You are taken to the learners list filtered to that batch, where you can review and manage each enrolled learner.

task: Delete a batch
content_text: Deleting a batch removes it permanently and cannot be undone. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches and select the session. 2. On the batch card you want to remove, click the trash icon in the top-right corner. 3. In the 'Delete Batch' confirmation dialog, click 'Yes, Delete'. 4. A 'Batch deleted successfully' toast confirms the removal.

task: Create an academic session
content_text: A session is an academic period (e.g. a year or semester) that batches belong to. Note: you must have at least one course first. 1. Open Manage Institute > Sessions from the sidebar. 2. Click 'Add New Session'. 3. Enter a 'Session Name' (e.g. 2024-2025) and a 'Start Date'. 4. Under 'Select levels from courses', tick the level checkboxes for each course you want this session to cover (use 'Add course' if you need a new one). 5. Click 'Add'. 6. A 'Session added successfully' toast confirms it.

task: Edit or delete an academic session
content_text: Manage existing sessions from the Sessions page. 1. Open Manage Institute > Sessions from the sidebar. 2. Find the session card you want to change. 3. Click the three-dots menu (top right of the card). 4. Choose 'Edit Session' to change the name, start date, or selected course levels, then click 'Save Changes'. 5. Or choose 'Delete Session' and confirm to remove it.

task: Set up a package (course, level and session)
content_text: A package is the combination of a Course, a Level, and a Session - it's created automatically when you make a batch. 1. Open Manage Institute > Batches and click 'Create Batch'. 2. Step 1: pick or create the Course. 3. Step 2: pick or create the Session. 4. Step 3: pick or create the Level. 5. Click 'Create Batch' - this Course + Level + Session combination is the package, now ready for enrollment.

task: Browse and filter all packages
content_text: Admins can review every Course + Level + Session package in one table. 1. From the sidebar open Manage Institute > Manage Packages. 2. The Package Management table lists all packages. 3. Use the search box to find a package by name. 4. Use the Session, Level, and Status filters to narrow the list. 5. Click a package row to open its details sidebar.

task: Add a team member or admin to the institute
content_text: Manage your institute's staff and admins from the Teams page. 1. Open Manage Institute > Teams from the sidebar. 2. The 'Institute Users' tab lists current members; the 'Invites' tab shows pending invitations. 3. Click the invite-users action (top right) to add a new person and assign their role. 4. Switch to the 'Invites' tab to track who hasn't accepted yet.

task: Build a reporting org chart for your team
content_text: The Org Chart visualises who reports to whom (this tab is enabled per-institute, so it may be hidden). 1. Open Manage Institute > Teams and click the 'Org Chart' tab. 2. Create or select a team from the dropdown. 3. Add a person via the 'Add a person' dialog: pick someone from your institute, optionally give them a role label, and choose who they report to. 4. Drag a person's card onto a manager's card to change their reporting line. 5. Leaving 'Reports to' blank places them at the top of the team.

task: Create a new assessment
content_text: Start a new assessment by choosing the type that matches how it should run. 1) Open the Assessments page from the sidebar (the 'Create Assessment' screen). 2) Pick a type card: Examination (fixed-time, scheduled), Mock Assessment (always available, fixed duration), Practice Assessment (on-demand, no time limit), Survey (no right/wrong answers), or Manual Upload Exam (solve offline and upload answer sheets). 3) You are taken into the assessment builder. 4) Complete the four steps shown at the top: Basic Info, Add Questions, Add Participants, Access Control. 5) On the last step click Save (or Update) and then Publish.

task: Fill in assessment basic info (step 1)
content_text: Step 1 of the builder captures the core details of the assessment. 1) In the Add Questions builder, make sure you are on the Basic Info step. 2) Enter the Assessment Name (for a Survey this field reads Survey Name). 3) Set the schedule and time-limit options shown on the form. 4) Add any instructions or description using the rich text editor. 5) Click Save (or Update) to move to the Add Questions step.

task: Add questions to an assessment
content_text: Step 2 organizes questions into sections. 1) On the Add Questions step, a default 'Section 1' is created; use Add Section to add more. 2) Within a section, click Upload Question Paper > Upload from Device to import a file, or Create Manually to type questions in. 3) You can also generate questions with AI from within the section. 4) Set the section duration (or enable Section-Wise duration) where required. 5) Save to continue to Add Participants.

task: Import or upload a question paper
content_text: Question papers are managed under Questions Banks and Papers in the sidebar. 1) Open the Question Papers page ('Question Paper Access & Management'). 2) Click Add Question Paper. 3) Choose Create Manually to enter questions yourself, or Upload from Device to import a file. 4) Fill the title and subject/tag details. 5) Save the paper so it is available to reuse when building assessments.

task: Generate evaluation criteria with AI for questions
content_text: AI can draft grading rubrics for subjective questions in a section. 1) On the Add Questions step, open the section that contains the questions. 2) Use the AI option to let AI draft evaluation rubrics for every question. 3) Click Generate All Criteria to create criteria for all questions in the section at once. 4) Review the generated criteria per question and edit as needed before saving.

task: Add participants to an assessment
content_text: Step 3 defines who can take the assessment. 1) On the Add Participants step, choose how to add people. 2) Use Batch Selection to add whole batches, or Individual Selection to pick specific students. 3) Select the sections/batches that apply. 4) Save to continue to Access Control.

task: Set access control and publish an assessment
content_text: Step 4 controls who can manage, view results, and evaluate the assessment, then publishes it. 1) On the Access Control step, set the Assessment Creation Access and the users allowed to manage, view results, and evaluate. 2) Click Save (or Update) to store your settings. 3) Click Publish to make the assessment live to its participants according to its schedule.

task: Find and manage scheduled tests
content_text: Scheduled Tests lists all your assessments by status. 1) Open Scheduled Tests from the Assessments and Tests group in the sidebar. 2) Switch between the Live, Upcoming, Previous, and Drafts tabs to find a test. 3) Use the search and filters to narrow the list. 4) Open the '...' menu on a test and choose View Assessment Details, or Delete Assessment to remove it.

task: View submissions and results for an assessment
content_text: Submissions and results live under Test Evaluations. 1) Open Test Evaluations from the Assessments and Tests group in the sidebar. 2) Click the assessment you want to review. 3) On its submissions page, switch between Internal Participants and External Participants. 4) For internal, toggle Batch Selection or Individual Selection. 5) Review the Attempted, Pending, and Ongoing participants to see who has submitted and their status.

task: Evaluate a student's submission
content_text: Evaluators grade submitted responses from the evaluations area. 1) Open Test Evaluations from the sidebar and click the assessment. 2) From the participants list, open a submitted (Attempted) student's response. 3) The 'Evaluate Response' screen opens with the response file. 4) Review the answers and assign marks per question. 5) Save to record the evaluation. Use the bulk actions menu to act on multiple submissions at once.

task: Evaluate scanned answer sheets
content_text: Scanned answer sheets are graded with the dedicated evaluation tool. 1) Open Scanned Answer sheet Evaluation from the Assessments and Tests group in the sidebar. 2) On the 'Evaluate Answer Sheets' screen, start a new evaluation. 3) Load the scanned answer-sheet file. 4) Use the on-screen tools to mark and annotate the PDF. 5) Assign marks and save the evaluation.

task: Enter marks manually for an offline (Manual Upload) exam
content_text: For a Manual Upload Exam, where students solve offline, you enter marks per student. 1) Create the assessment using the Manual Upload Exam type. 2) From Scheduled Tests, open the assessment to reach its Offline Data Entry screen. 3) Select a student. 4) Enter their responses or marks per question. 5) Submit to save the student's results, then repeat for the next student.

task: Invite a team member (admin, content creator, evaluator, etc.)
content_text: Invite staff into your institute with a role from the Dashboard. 1. Open the Dashboard. 2. On the "Role Type Users" card, click "Manage Users". 3. In the "Manage Role Types Users" window, click "Invite Users" (top right). 4. Enter the person's Full Name and Email. 5. Open the "Role Type" dropdown and select one or more roles (e.g. Admin, Content Creator, Assessment Creator, Evaluator). 6. Click "Invite User". They appear under the "Invites" tab until they accept.

task: Invite a teacher and assign them to a batch and subjects
content_text: Inviting a teacher requires picking the batches and subjects they will handle. 1. Open the Dashboard and click "Manage Users" on the "Role Type Users" card. 2. Click "Invite Users". 3. Enter Full Name and Email. 4. In "Role Type", select Teacher (Teacher cannot be combined with other roles). 5. A "Select Batch and Subjects" card appears - search for and tick each batch. 6. For every ticked batch, choose at least one subject under "Select Subjects". 7. Click "Invite User".

task: Assign an existing teacher to a batch and subject from a course
content_text: Assign faculty to a batch/subject directly from inside a course. 1. Open the course from your Courses library and go to the Teacher tab (or open a subject). 2. Click "Invite Users" to open the "Add Team Member" dialog. 3. Stay on the "Existing Members" tab and search for the staff member, then select them. 4. In "Select Batch and Subjects", tick the batch(es) and pick at least one subject each. 5. Click "Assign <name>". Use the "Invite New" tab instead if the teacher is not yet in your institute.

task: Change a user's role
content_text: Update which roles an existing user holds. 1. Open the Dashboard and click "Manage Users". 2. On the "Institute Users" tab, find the user (you can filter by Role Type or Status). 3. Click the three-dots (...) menu on their row. 4. Choose "Change Role Type". 5. In the "Change Roles" dialog, adjust the selected roles in the "Role Type" dropdown. 6. Click "Submit".

task: Disable, enable, or delete a team member
content_text: Deactivate or remove staff access from the user list. 1. Open the Dashboard and click "Manage Users". 2. On the "Institute Users" tab, find the user and click the three-dots (...) menu. 3. Choose "Disable user" to revoke access (or "Enable user" to restore a disabled account, or "Delete user" to remove them). 4. Confirm by clicking "Yes" in the confirmation dialog.

task: Create a custom role
content_text: Create a custom role beyond the built-in ones (Admin, Teacher, etc.). 1. Go to Settings and open the Role Display tab. 2. At the top, switch the role selector to "Custom Role". 3. Click the "+" button next to the role dropdown. 4. Type a role name and click "Create". 5. The new role is added and can be selected to configure its permissions and visibility.

task: Assign permissions and page visibility to a role
content_text: Control what a role can see and do via Role Display Settings. 1. Go to Settings and open the Role Display tab. 2. Use the selector at the top to choose Admin, Teacher, or Custom Role (pick a specific custom role from its dropdown). 3. Work through the section groups: "Courses & Permissions" (e.g. can edit/delete course structure, can copy/move/delete slides), "Layout & Navigation" (sidebar tabs, categories), "Content & Learners", and "Dashboard & Access". 4. Toggle each switch on or off. 5. Click Save (or use the unsaved-changes bar) to apply.

task: Assign a custom role to a new team member with course access
content_text: Add a member under a custom role with specific course/session access via Custom Teams. 1. Open the Manage Custom Teams page. 2. Click "Add Member". 3. Fill in Full Name, Email, and Phone. 4. In "Role", pick an existing role or choose "+ Custom Role" and type a new role name. 5. Under "User Access Mapping", search and tick one or more Package Sessions to grant access. 6. Optionally set the Linkage Type (Direct, Inherited, Partnership). 7. Click "Add Member".

task: View a team member's access and permissions
content_text: See exactly which courses, sessions, and permissions a member has. 1. Open the Manage Custom Teams page. 2. In the members table, click the member's row. 3. The "Access Details" dialog lists each access mapping with its Type (Course, Session, Invite, or Global), Name, granted Permissions, and Linkage. If nothing is mapped, it shows "No special access mapped for this user."

task: Create a sub-organization (custom team)
content_text: Set up a sub-organization to manage a partner team and its allowed roles. 1. Open the Manage Custom Teams page. 2. Click "Create Sub-Organization". 3. On Step 1, enter the Name and the other organization details. 4. Continue through the steps to assign package sessions and (optionally) restrict which team roles the sub-org admin may use. 5. Finish to create it. The new sub-org appears in the list with an invite link you can copy.

task: Find team members invited but not yet active
content_text: Check which invites are still pending acceptance. 1. Open the Dashboard and click "Manage Users". 2. Switch to the "Invites" tab - the badge shows the count of pending invites. 3. Use the "Role Type" filter to narrow the list, then click Apply. Members move to the "Institute Users" tab once they accept and become active.

task: Rename roles shown across the portal (e.g. Teacher to Instructor)
content_text: Role labels shown in dropdowns and lists come from your institute's Naming Settings. 1. Go to Settings and open the Naming Settings tab. 2. Find the role term you want to change (Admin, Teacher, Course Creator, Assessment Creator, Evaluator, Learner). 3. Enter your preferred singular and plural labels. 4. Save - the new wording appears wherever that role is displayed, including the invite and Manage Users screens.

task: Schedule a single live session
content_text: Create one live class with date, time, streaming link, and participants. 1) In the sidebar open the Live Sessions group and click 'Create new' (or open Scheduled Live Sessions and click the 'Schedule' button). 2) On the 'Schedule Live Sessions' page fill the 'Basic Information' card: enter a Title and pick a Subject. 3) In the 'Schedule' card set the date, time and timezone (choose one-time or recurring). 4) In 'Streaming & Link' choose the live-stream platform and meeting link. 5) Optionally configure 'Waiting Room & Playback' and 'Learner Feedback'. 6) Click Next to continue to the Participants step. 7) On 'Participants & Access' pick who can join, then click 'Preview & create' to finish.

task: Bulk-schedule many live sessions at once
content_text: Add many independent (non-recurring) classes in one grid; each row becomes one session. 1) In the sidebar open the Live Sessions group and click 'Bulk Schedule'. 2) On the 'Bulk Schedule' page add a row per class, filling its title, date, time, link and participants. 3) To start from a spreadsheet, click 'Template' to download the CSV format, fill it in, then click 'Import CSV' to load the rows. 4) Review the grid, then submit to create all sessions. Note: recurring weekly classes can't be created here — use the single-class 'Create new' flow for those.

task: Set who can join a live session and notifications
content_text: On the second scheduling step you choose the audience and notifications for a live class. 1) After completing the Basic Information step, you land on the 'Participants & Access' page. 2) In 'Participant Access' choose whether access is open or restricted. 3) In 'Select Participants' pick the batches or individual learners who can attend. 4) Optionally enable a 'Registration Form' and set the 'Join Link'. 5) In the 'Notifications' card choose which reminders go out and set 'Notify Before' timings. 6) Click 'Preview & create' to review and publish the session (use 'Back' to return to the details step).

task: View live class attendance
content_text: See attendance across your live classes with filters and CSV export. 1) In the sidebar open the Live Sessions group and click 'Live Session Attendance' (the Attendance Tracker page titled 'Live Class Attendance'). 2) Use the 'Select Session' dropdown, the date-range filter and the Batch dropdown to narrow the list. 3) Type in 'Search students...' to find a specific learner. 4) Read the Present/Absent status per learner in the table. 5) Click 'Clear Filters' to reset, or 'Export CSV' to download the report.

task: Mark or edit attendance for a session
content_text: Mark each learner present or absent for a specific live session. 1) In the sidebar open the Live Sessions group, click 'Scheduled Live Sessions', and open the session you want (this opens the session view). 2) Scroll to the 'Attendance' card. For a one-off session click the 'View Attendance' button (it toggles to 'Hide Attendance'); for a recurring session pick the past session date tile you want to mark. 3) The attendance table loads. For each learner click the 'P' (green check) button to mark Present or the 'A' (red X) button to mark Absent; clicking the active state again clears it back to 'Unmarked'. 4) Watch the Present / Absent / Unmarked summary counts (and Total) update at the top. 5) Click 'Save Attendance (N changed)' to record your edits, or 'Discard changes' to revert. 6) Use 'Export CSV' to download the marked list.

task: Rename terminology (Course, Batch, Learner, etc.)
content_text: Customize the words used across your institute (e.g. Course to Product, Learner to Student). 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Naming Settings' tab. 2) Terms are grouped into 'Content & Structure', 'User Roles' and 'Other'. 3) For each term, type your custom word in the 'Singular' field and its plural in the 'Plural' field (placeholders show the system default). 4) Use the per-row reset icon, or 'Reset All', to revert to defaults. 5) Click 'Save Changes' to apply — the new names appear throughout the app and sidebar.

task: Set up payment plans for a course
content_text: Create the payment plans learners use at checkout. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Payment Settings' tab. 2) Set the 'Default Currency' for your institute. 3) In the 'Payment Configuration' card click 'Add Payment Plan'. 4) Choose a plan type (e.g. Free, Subscription, Upfront, Donation) and fill in its pricing details. 5) Configure any discounts and approval options for the plan. 6) Save the plan — it then appears in your list and can be attached to courses. Note: connect a payment gateway in the 'Payment Gateways' tab so charges can actually be processed.

task: Create a discount coupon
content_text: Make discount codes learners apply at checkout. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Coupon Settings' tab. 2) Turn on the enable toggle so coupons show at checkout (you can create them while off, but learners won't see them). 3) Click 'Create coupon'. 4) Enter the 'Coupon code' (e.g. SAVE20) and pick a 'Discount type' — Percentage (%) or Flat amount. 5) Enter the 'Value', and for a percentage set a 'Max cap'. 6) Optionally set start/end dates, a usage limit, and restrict it to specific batches, invite links or allowed emails. 7) Save the coupon.

task: Configure and auto-issue certificates
content_text: Design a certificate and automatically award it on course completion. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Certificate Settings' tab. 2) Upload a certificate background image and drag dynamic fields (name, course, date) onto it. 3) Choose the aspect ratio. 4) Turn on 'Auto-issue certificates' and set the completion threshold at which the certificate is granted. 5) Click 'Save Changes'. Tip: set your institute logo and theme color in Dashboard → Edit institute profile so they appear on issued certificates.

task: Connect an ad lead integration (Meta / Google)
content_text: Pull leads from Meta or Google ad lead forms into your audiences. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Ad Integrations' tab ('Ad Platform Integrations'). 2) In the 'Add New Connector' card click 'Meta Lead Ads' or 'Google Lead Forms'. 3) For Meta, click 'Connect Meta Account' and authorize, then pick the Facebook Page and lead form, and map its fields to your audience custom fields. 4) For Google, follow the form fields and copy the generated webhook URL into your Google form. 5) Save the connector. 6) Use the 'Test connection' action on a connector to verify leads can flow (if it shows ACTION_REQUIRED, grant Full control on the page and use 'Re-subscribe').

task: Connect a payment gateway (Razorpay, Stripe, etc.)
content_text: Add the vendor that actually processes learner payments. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Payment Gateways' tab. 2) Click 'Add Gateway'. 3) Choose a Vendor (Stripe, Razorpay, PhonePe, Cashfree or Eway). 4) Enter that vendor's required keys/credentials in the fields shown. 5) Save — the gateway appears under 'Configured gateways'. Keys are stored per institute, so configure this before relying on paid payment plans.

task: Adjust live session scheduling options
content_text: Control which live-session features and platforms are available when scheduling. 1) Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'Live Session Settings' tab. 2) Under 'Allowed Streaming Platforms', toggle which platforms admins can pick. 3) Toggle defaults such as recurring weekly schedule, default class link for the day, daily attendance counting, the description field, learner feedback (and whether it's compulsory), the custom action button, and the recording/transcription button. 4) Click 'Save changes'. Note: changes apply only to new live classes scheduled after saving — existing classes are unaffected.

task: View the institute dashboard and its KPIs
content_text: The Dashboard is your home screen with at-a-glance metrics and quick actions. 1. In the left sidebar (CRM section), click Dashboard. 2. The KPI band at the top shows tiles such as Total Learners, Total Courses, Team Members, Outstanding Fees, Overdue Items, and Live Sessions Today (terms reflect your institute's naming). 3. Click any KPI tile to open the related section via its deep link. 4. Scroll down to see widgets like Pending Actions, Live Classes, Recent Notifications, and Unresolved Doubts. Note: if your role hides the CRM section, you are redirected to your role's landing page instead.

task: View the Sales Dashboard for pipeline and counsellor performance
content_text: The Sales Dashboard summarizes pipeline, follow-ups, campaigns, and counsellor performance. 1. In the sidebar, expand Leads and click Sales Dashboard. 2. Use the date range pills (7d, 30d, 90d, or Custom) at the top right to set the window; for Custom, pick a start and end date. 3. If you manage teams, use the Team picker to scope the data. 4. Review the KPI band, then the Conversion Funnel and Counsellor Leaderboard, Upcoming/Missed Follow-ups, and campaign cards below. Note: if you see 'Sales dashboard is not enabled', turn it on under Lead Settings > Workbench.

task: Browse and filter recent leads
content_text: Recent Leads lists incoming leads with rich filters. 1. In the sidebar, expand Leads and click Recent Leads. 2. Use the filter row to narrow by tier (All tiers / Hot / Warm / Cold), status (Active leads / All statuses), action status (SLA), audience (All audiences), counsellor, and source. 3. Set a date range using the From / To controls. 4. Type in the Search leads box to find a lead by name. 5. Click Clear all to reset filters. 6. Click Export to download the filtered leads as a CSV.

task: Assign a counsellor to a lead
content_text: Assign or reassign a lead to a counsellor from the leads list. 1. Open Leads > Recent Leads in the sidebar. 2. Trigger the assign action for that lead in any of these ways: in the Counsellor column click the dashed 'Assign' button (person-plus icon) if it is unassigned, or the reassign (circular-arrows) icon next to the current owner; OR click the person-plus 'Assign counsellor' quick-action icon in the row's action buttons; OR open the row's actions (three-dot) menu and choose 'Assign counsellor'. 3. In the 'Assign Counselor' dialog, type a name in the 'Search Counselor' box ('Type to search by name...'). 4. Pick the person from the results (they appear under 'Selected Counselor'). 5. Click 'Assign' to save (it shows 'Assigning...' while it saves). The same Assign Counselor dialog is also available from the Follow-ups screen and the campaign/audience leads tables.

task: Work through your daily follow-ups
content_text: The Follow-ups screen surfaces the leads you owe a touch today. 1. In the sidebar, expand Leads and click Follow-ups. 2. Use the bucket cards to switch between Pending (overdue), Today, Upcoming, and All. 3. Toggle between List and Calendar views using the tabs. 4. Use the inline Mark complete action on a row to close out a follow-up. 5. Admins can use the counsellor filter at the top right to view another counsellor's follow-ups (defaults to your own).

task: View lead and sales reports
content_text: The Reports Center gives pipeline health, sources, funnel, calling and follow-up reports in one place. 1. In the sidebar, expand Leads and click Reports. 2. Set the window with the date preset pills or the From / To date inputs, then click Apply (Reset clears it). 3. Optionally scope by Team and counsellor using the pickers on the right of the filter bar. 4. Switch tabs across Overview, Sources, Funnel, Dispositions, Calling, Activity, Follow-ups, Counsellors, and Manager. 5. Click Refresh to pull the latest data.

task: Manage your audience lists and campaigns
content_text: The Lead List screen is where you create and share audience campaigns that capture leads. 1. In the sidebar, expand Leads and click Lead List. 2. Use the status filter chips and the Sub-Org filter to narrow the campaigns shown, or click a KPI tile to filter. 3. Click Add (the create button at the top right) to open the Create Campaign dialog and fill in name, type, and form fields. 4. On any campaign card use Add Response to enter a response manually, API for integration details, or Embed to get website embed code.

task: Manage admission enquiries
content_text: The Enquiries screen lists enquiry-form responses with status and source filters. 1. In the sidebar, expand Admissions and click Enquiries. 2. Pick an enquiry form from the Select Enquiry dropdown at the top. 3. Filter responses with the Status, Source, and date-range controls, and search by name or mobile. 4. Click Add New Enquiry Response to record a response on the selected form, or Copy Form Link to share the public form. 5. Use Walk-in Register (when lead system is enabled) to capture a walk-in on the spot.

task: Bulk import enquiry responses from a CSV
content_text: Import many enquiry responses at once from a spreadsheet. 1. Open Admissions > Enquiries in the sidebar and select an enquiry form. 2. Open the Bulk Import Enquiry Responses dialog. 3. Click Download Template to get the CSV format, then fill in the rows. 4. Upload your filled CSV and optionally choose a class. 5. Preview the parsed rows, then confirm to import; the result shows how many responses succeeded and failed.

task: Enable the lead management system and configure scoring
content_text: Lead management (scoring, tiers, workbench) is turned on and tuned from Settings. 1. Open Settings from the sidebar. 2. Select the Lead Settings tab. 3. On the Configuration sub-tab, toggle the Lead Management System on or off. 4. Adjust Scoring Weights, Recency Decay, and Score Badge Visibility to shape how leads are tiered. 5. Use the Workbench sub-tab to enable counsellor-facing tools such as the Sales Dashboard.

task: Create a counsellor pool for auto-assigning leads
content_text: Counselor Pools auto-assign incoming leads to counsellors on a schedule and order. 1. Open Settings, select the Lead Settings tab, then the Pools sub-tab. 2. Click Create Pool (this opens the full-page pool editor). 3. On the Overview tab name the pool and save. 4. Use the Audiences tab to choose which audiences feed the pool, and the Counselors tab to add members. 5. Set rotation in the Order tab and availability in the Schedule tab. Tip: each pool card also lets you edit an existing pool.

task: Control which tools the Vacademy Assistant can use
content_text: You decide which Assistant capabilities your team can use. 1. Open Settings from the sidebar. 2. Select the 'Vacademy Assistant' tab. 3. Under 'Capabilities for everyone', toggle the tools available to all non-learner roles. 4. Use 'Per-role access (advanced)' to grant extra tools to specific roles. 5. Click 'Save settings'.

task: Edit institute name, logo and contact details
content_text: Update your institute's name, logo, and contact information from the Dashboard. 1. Open Dashboard from the sidebar. 2. Click Edit Institute (or Add Details on the "Complete your institute profile" card). 3. In the Edit Institute dialog, click the pencil icon on the logo image to upload a new institute logo. 4. Edit Institute Name and choose an Institute Type. 5. Under Contact Information, set Institute Email, Institute Phone Number, and Institute Website. 6. Under Location Details, fill in Address, City/Village, State, Country, and Pincode. 7. Click Save Changes.

task: Change the institute theme color
content_text: Set the primary brand color used across the admin portal. 1. Open Dashboard from the sidebar. 2. Click Edit Institute (or Add Details on the profile card). 3. Scroll to the Institute Theme section and click Change Theme. 4. In the Select Theme dialog, pick a preset color (Orange, Blue, Green, Purple, Red, Pink, Indigo, Yellow, or Cyan) — the selected swatch is highlighted. 5. Click Save to close the theme picker. 6. Back on the Edit Institute dialog, click Save Changes to apply.

task: Set up a white-label custom domain
content_text: Configure custom domains for your learner, admin, and teacher portals. 1. Go to Settings and open the White-Label Setup tab. 2. Under New Setup, for each entry pick a Role (Learner / Admin / Teacher Portal) and enter a Domain (e.g. learn.myschool.com or my-school.vacademy.io). 3. Mark one entry per role as Primary using the star. 4. Click Add another domain for additional entries. 5. Click Settings on an entry to expand branding, authentication, routes, and links options. 6. Click Apply White-Label Setup (or Update White-Label Setup). Note: this requires Cloudflare to be enabled on your deployment; otherwise the tab shows "Not available on this deployment".

task: Customize white-label portal branding and login options
content_text: Fine-tune branding, login methods, and links for each white-labeled portal domain. 1. Go to Settings > White-Label Setup. 2. On a domain entry, click Settings to expand its config. 3. Under Branding, set the Tab Title, upload a Tab Icon, set Theme/Color and Font Family; toggle Hide institute name and set Logo width/height (px) if needed. 4. Under Authentication, toggle which login methods to allow (Sign Up, Google, GitHub, Email OTP, Phone, Username/Password). 5. Under Phone Input Preferences, choose Preferred Countries (first one is the default). 6. Under Routes and Links, set redirect/after-login routes, Privacy Policy and Terms URLs, and app store links. 7. Click Apply / Update White-Label Setup to save.

task: Configure assessment report branding (logo, colors, letterhead)
content_text: Brand the PDF reports generated for students. 1. Go to Settings and open the Assessment Settings tab. 2. Scroll to Assessment Report Branding. 3. Under Report Colors, set the Primary and Secondary colors using the color picker or hex field. 4. Under Logo & Letterhead, click Upload Logo (crop, then it uploads) and toggle Show Logo in Header. 5. Optionally Upload Letterhead for a full-page background and toggle Show Letterhead Background; use Crop to adjust. 6. Save your Assessment Settings to apply across all assessment reports.

task: Add a watermark, header, and footer to assessment reports
content_text: Add a diagonal watermark and custom header/footer to report PDFs. 1. Go to Settings > Assessment Settings and scroll to Assessment Report Branding. 2. Under Watermark, toggle Enable Watermark, enter Watermark Text (e.g. CONFIDENTIAL), and adjust the Opacity slider. 3. Under Header & Footer, enter Footer Text and optional Custom Header HTML / Custom Footer HTML — use the {{assessment_name}} placeholder to insert the assessment name. 4. Under Preview, click Show Preview to check the layout. 5. Save your Assessment Settings.

task: Open the doubt and query inbox
content_text: Open the unified inbox where learner doubts and queries land for your institute. 1. In the left sidebar, under LMS, click Doubt Management. 2. The inbox opens with the list of doubts on the left and the selected conversation on the right. 3. The list is scoped to your current institute, so general (batchless) queries appear alongside in-course doubts. 4. Click any item in the left list to load its full conversation in the right pane. 5. On a phone, the list fills the screen; tapping a doubt swaps to the conversation, and the back arrow returns to the list.

task: Reply to a learner doubt or query
content_text: Answer a learner's doubt from the conversation pane. 1. Open Doubt Management from the sidebar (under LMS). 2. Select a doubt in the left list to load its thread on the right. 3. The header shows the learner's name and category; the original question and any earlier replies appear above. 4. Type your answer in the "Type your reply..." box at the bottom; use the expand button for the full Compose Reply editor if you need more room. 5. Click the paper-plane send button to post your reply. Note: replying is open to admins and teachers; other staff can reply only to doubts assigned to them.

task: Mark a doubt as resolved
content_text: Close out a doubt once it's answered so it drops off the unresolved list. 1. Open Doubt Management and select the doubt in the left list. 2. In the conversation header on the right, toggle the Mark as Resolved switch on. 3. The label changes to Resolved and the learner is notified (per your resolved-event notification settings). 4. To reopen, toggle the same switch back off — its status returns to Unresolved. Tip: use the Status filter at the top to switch between All, Resolved, and Unresolved views.

task: Filter the doubt inbox by status, type, batch, or date
content_text: Narrow the inbox to find the doubts you care about. 1. Open Doubt Management; the Filters bar sits above the inbox. 2. Status: choose All, Resolved, or Unresolved. 3. Type: pick one or more query types (Doubt, Technical Issue, Payment Issue, or your custom types); All clears it. 4. Batch: pick one or more batches, or leave on All to include general batchless queries. 5. Date: choose Today, This Week, This Month, This Year, or Custom (pick a start and end date, then Apply). 6. The left list updates as you change filters.

task: Assign a doubt to a teacher or staff member
content_text: Manually assign (or reassign) who handles a specific doubt. 1. Open Doubt Management and select the doubt. 2. In the conversation pane, find the Assigned row directly under the header (visible to admins). 3. Click the assignee control to pick a teacher or staff member from your institute. 4. For doubts that route to subject or batch teachers, the mapped faculty appear pre-selected as defaults; for other types only your explicit picks show. 5. The chosen handler can then reply to the doubt from their own inbox.

task: Jump to the slide a doubt was raised on
content_text: Open the exact slide a learner was on when they raised a doubt, for context before replying. 1. Open Doubt Management and select a doubt. 2. If the doubt was raised on a slide, a view-source navigate control appears in the conversation header. 3. Click it to jump to that slide in the course. 4. For video slides, the raised timestamp is shown on the original question so you know exactly where in the video the learner had trouble. Note: this only appears for slide-sourced doubts, not for general queries.

task: Delete a doubt
content_text: Permanently remove a doubt (for example spam or a duplicate). Admin only. 1. Open Doubt Management and select the doubt. 2. In the conversation header on the right, click the delete (trash) action. 3. Confirm in the Delete Doubt dialog by clicking Delete. 4. The doubt and its replies are removed from the inbox. Note: this control is shown only to admins; teachers can reply and resolve but not delete.

task: Configure query types and where they route
content_text: Define the categories learners can choose and who each one routes to. 1. Go to Settings, then open the Doubt Management tab. 2. Scroll to the Query types card. The built-in Doubt type can't be removed and uses your Default auto-assignment. 3. Click Add query type and name it (e.g. Technical Issue, Payment Issue). 4. Per type, toggle Enabled and "Learner can pick". 5. Under "Route to", choose Subject teacher, Batch teacher, A role, Specific staff, or No auto-assign (manual). For A role, pick the role; for Specific staff, select the handlers. 6. Click Save settings.

task: Set the default auto-assignment for new doubts
content_text: Control who gets pre-assigned when a learner raises a new academic doubt. 1. Go to Settings, then open the Doubt Management tab. 2. In the Default auto-assignment card, pick one: Subject teacher, Batch teacher, Both, or None (manual). 3. If you pick Subject teacher, a Fallback behavior card appears — toggle whether to fall back to batch teachers when a subject has no mapped faculty. 4. Click Save settings. Note: this affects only new doubts; existing doubts keep their current assignees.

task: Enable learner query intake and its entry points
content_text: Let learners (and optionally logged-out visitors) raise queries from outside a course. 1. Go to Settings, then open the Doubt Management tab. 2. In the Learner query intake card, turn on "Enable learner query intake" (the master switch). 3. Optionally turn on "Show '?' icon in the top bar" and "Show 'Raise a query' card on the dashboard". 4. Optionally turn on "Allow logged-out visitors to raise queries" to add a Need help? button to the learner login page; staff replies are emailed to the visitor. 5. Click Save settings. Note: learners only see query types you marked "Learner can pick".

task: Configure doubt notifications (push, email, in-app bell)
content_text: Choose how staff and learners are notified about doubt events. 1. Go to Settings, then open the Doubt Management tab. 2. "When a doubt is raised" notifies the assigned teacher(s); "When a doubt is resolved" notifies the learner. Each card has Push, In-app bell alert, and Email toggles. 3. For Email, leave the template as Default (auto) to use the seeded institute template, or pick a custom one. 4. Click Save settings. Important: push only reaches a device after that admin/teacher clicks Enable push in the "Push notifications on this device" card and grants browser permission.

task: Send an announcement or notification to learners and staff
content_text: Compose and send an announcement across in-app modes, push, email and WhatsApp from the Communications section. 1. In the sidebar open Communications and click Create Announcement. 2. Enter a Title, optional Preview Text, and write the message in Content (toggle between Rich Editor and HTML Source, and use Preview to see how the email looks). 3. Under Recipients, add an audience using + All Students, + All Teachers, + Add Batch, the Add button (Role/User/Tag), or + Campaign. 4. Under Modes, pick where it appears (use the General Announcement or Pinned Update preset, or toggle individual mode cards) and fill any required mode settings. 5. Under Mediums, tick the channels to deliver on (PUSH_NOTIFICATION, EMAIL, WHATSAPP); for EMAIL choose a From Email and optionally Use Email Template. 6. Under Scheduling choose IMMEDIATE, ONE_TIME or RECURRING (set a run time or cron, and a timezone). 7. Click Create Announcement, or Review and Create to confirm details first.

task: Schedule an announcement to send later
content_text: Set an announcement to go out at a future time or on a repeating schedule. 1. Open Communications then Create Announcement and fill in the Title, Content, Recipients, Modes and Mediums. 2. Scroll to the Scheduling section and open the Schedule Type dropdown. 3. Choose ONE_TIME and set Run at (date and time), or choose RECURRING and enter a Cron Expression (use the Every day at 9 AM / Every Monday 9 AM / Every hour templates, or the Now / Later today 5pm / Tomorrow 9am quick picks). 4. Pick the Timezone the schedule should be interpreted in. 5. Click Create Announcement to save the scheduled send.

task: View and manage scheduled announcements on the calendar
content_text: See planned announcements on a calendar and edit, cancel or schedule new ones. 1. In the sidebar open Communications and click Schedule Announcement. 2. Switch between Day, 3-day, Week and Month views and use Prev, Today and Next to move through dates; filter by mode with the mode dropdown. 3. Click Schedule new (or Schedule here on a day) to open Create Announcement prefilled for that date. 4. On an upcoming event card, click Edit to change it or Delete to cancel and remove it (you confirm before deleting). 5. For an item Pending Approval, use Approve or Reject (Reject lets you enter a reason).

task: Review past announcements and delivery stats
content_text: Look back at sent and planned announcements and check delivery, read and email open rates. 1. In the sidebar open Communications and click Announcement History. 2. Use the All, Planned and Past tabs, and the Search title box, Status filter and date range to narrow the list. 3. Click View on a row to see its content, modes, mediums and recipients. 4. Click Stats to open Delivery overview (Recipients, Delivered, Read, Failed) plus Email events such as Opened, Clicked and Bounced. 5. For a scheduled/one-time item, click Deliver now to send immediately; use Approve or Reject on items Pending Approval, or Delete to remove.

task: Run an email campaign to learners
content_text: Send a focused email campaign using your institute's from-addresses and templates. 1. In the sidebar open Communications and click Email Campaigning. 2. Enter a Campaign name and write the email content in the editor (use the device Preview to check how it renders). 3. Choose the recipients (Role, Batch, User, Tag or Campaign audience). 4. Pick a From Email, and optionally turn on Use Email Template and select a saved template. 5. Set the schedule (IMMEDIATE, ONE_TIME or RECURRING) and timezone. 6. Submit to send or schedule the campaign.

task: Configure notification channels and who can send announcements
content_text: Set institute-wide rules for announcements, in-app messages and each notification channel. 1. Open Settings and select the Notification Settings tab. 2. Under General, toggle Approval required, and set Max announcements/day, Retention (days) and the Default timezone. 3. Use the per-channel cards to control who can post: System Alerts, Dashboard Pins, Direct Messages, Streams, Resources, Community and In-App Messages (toggles like Teachers can send / Admins can send / Students can send, plus auto-dismiss and limits). 4. Click Save Settings to apply your changes.

task: Enable push notifications with Firebase
content_text: Turn on mobile push notifications by connecting your Firebase service account. 1. Open Settings and select the Notification Settings tab. 2. Scroll to the Push Notifications (Firebase) card. 3. Paste your Firebase service account JSON into the credentials field (or paste a Base64-encoded version in the field below it). 4. Turn on Enable Push Notifications. 5. Click Validate & Save to validate the JSON and store the credentials.

task: Manage the email addresses announcements are sent from
content_text: Add or edit the from-addresses your institute uses to email learners and staff. 1. Open Settings and select the Notification Settings tab. 2. Scroll to the Email Addresses card. 3. In Add a new email address, enter the Email address, a Display name, choose What is this email for? (e.g. Announcements & general info, Support & help, or a custom purpose) and optional Notes, then click Add email address. 4. To change an existing one, edit its Email address, Display name or Notes and click Save changes (the purpose can't be changed after adding); use Remove to delete it. 5. Note: a newly added address must be verified by support before it can actually send.

task: Open the Notification Hub to track activity and inbox conversations
content_text: Use the Notification Hub for delivery stats, recent learner activity and inbound conversations. 1. In the sidebar open Communications and click Notification Hub. 2. Stay on the Overview tab for stats and recent learner activity. 3. Switch to the WhatsApp Inbox tab to read and reply to inbound WhatsApp conversations. 4. Switch to the Email Inbox tab to view and reply to email threads.

task: Upload an image to the email asset library
content_text: Add an image to your institute's shared email asset library so it can be reused in any email template. 1. Open the email template editor (Templates > create an Email template) at /templates/create. 2. In the builder toolbar, click the 'Assets' button to open the 'Institute Email Assets' window. 3. In that window, click 'Upload Image'. 4. Choose one or more image files (JPG, PNG, or GIF) from your computer. 5. Wait for the 'Uploading...' indicator to finish; a success toast confirms the upload. 6. The new image appears in the grid and is now shared across all email templates in your institute.

task: Find an image in the email asset library
content_text: Locate a previously uploaded image in your institute's email asset library. 1. Open the email template editor (Templates > create an Email template) at /templates/create. 2. Click the 'Assets' button in the builder toolbar to open the 'Institute Email Assets' window. 3. Type part of the image name into the 'Search images...' box at the top to filter the grid. 4. Optionally narrow results with the category dropdown next to the search box. 5. Click the refresh icon if you just uploaded a file and don't see it yet. 6. Each card shows the image name, category, and who uploaded it; the footer shows how many assets match.

task: Reuse an uploaded image in an email template
content_text: Insert an image you already uploaded into the email you are building, without uploading it again. 1. Open the email template editor (Templates > create an Email template) at /templates/create. 2. Position your cursor or select the image block where you want the picture. 3. Click the 'Assets' button to open the 'Institute Email Assets' window. 4. Use the search box or category dropdown to find the image. 5. Click the image card to select it; the window closes and the image is placed into your template. 6. Assets are shared institute-wide, so the same image can be reused across every email template.

task: Reuse saved media from the library in the video editor
content_text: Pick an image or video you previously added, so you don't re-upload it into your video. 1. Open the video in the editor at /vim/edit/<videoId>. 2. Open the media picker (for example, add a media overlay from the layers/properties panel). 3. The picker opens on the 'Library' tab, showing your institute's saved media as thumbnails. 4. Click a thumbnail to insert that media into the canvas. 5. To remove an item from the library, hover the thumbnail and click the trash (delete) icon. 6. Uploaded, stock, and AI-generated media are auto-saved here, so they appear in the Library for reuse later.

task: Upload or source new media in the video editor
content_text: Add a new image or video to your video from your computer, stock libraries, or AI generation. 1. Open the video in the editor at /vim/edit/<videoId>. 2. Open the media picker (for example, when adding a media overlay). 3. Use the 'Upload' tab and click 'Choose a file' to upload an image or video from your device. 4. Or use the 'Stock' tab: type a search term, optionally pick a provider (Pexels or Pixabay), click 'Search', then click a result to use it. 5. For an image, use the 'AI' tab: describe the image, click 'Generate', then 'Use this image'. 6. Whatever you upload, search, or generate is automatically saved to your Library for reuse.

task: Use the Instructor Copilot
content_text: The Instructor Copilot helps you plan, run, and review lectures with AI. 1. Open 'Instructor Copilot' from the AI section of the sidebar. 2. Stay on the 'Lecture' tab (the 'Assessment' tab is marked Coming soon). 3. Pick one of the three stage cards: 'Before Lecture' (plan and prepare), 'In Lecture' (record, upload, or present), or 'After Lecture' (analyze and review logs). 4. Before Lecture links out to the AI Lecture Planner, Take Attendance, and Create Lecture Presentation. 5. In Lecture lets you record or upload audio, or run a live interactive presentation. 6. After Lecture opens 'Previous Lecture Logs' or 'Analyse your lecture'. Use 'Back to Lecture Modes' to return to the stage cards.

task: Record or upload a lecture to generate AI notes and summary
content_text: Turn lecture audio into a transcript, notes, summary, and flashcards automatically. 1. In Instructor Copilot, open the 'In Lecture' card. 2. Choose 'Record Live Lecture' to record directly, or 'Upload Lecture' to add an existing audio file. 3. The audio is uploaded and transcribed; a status banner shows progress (Uploading, Creating transcription job, Transcribing). 4. When transcription completes, content generation starts automatically and a 'Content generation started successfully!' message appears. 5. Review the generated output in the tabs: Transcription, Notes, Summary, Flashcards, Quiz, Slides, Video Ideas, Homework, Classwork. 6. Use 'Cancel' or 'Back to Selection' to discard and start over.

task: Generate a quiz from a lecture transcript with AI
content_text: Auto-create quiz questions from a transcribed lecture. 1. Open a lecture log (from 'Previous Lecture Logs') or a freshly transcribed session in Instructor Copilot. 2. Go to the 'Quiz' tab. 3. In 'Generate Quiz from Transcript', set the 'Number of Questions' and enter a 'Class Level' (e.g. Grade 10) — both are required. 4. Choose a 'Question Type' (MCQS or MCQM) and a 'Language'. 5. Click 'Generate Quiz from Transcript'; results are polled and a 'Quiz generated successfully!' message appears when ready. 6. The quiz is saved to the log so it stays available on the Quiz tab.

task: Review past lecture logs and download notes or quiz as PDF
content_text: Browse previously generated lecture content and export it. 1. In Instructor Copilot, open the 'After Lecture' card, then 'Previous Lecture Logs'. 2. Each log card shows a title, date, and a 'Ready' or 'Processing' badge; click a card or 'View Details' to open it. 3. Switch between the Transcription, Notes, Summary, Quiz, Classwork, and Homework tabs. 4. On the Notes, Summary, Quiz, Classwork, or Homework tabs, click 'Download PDF' to export that content. 5. If content is still missing, use 'Regenerate Content' (subject to a short cooldown). 6. Use the trash icon on a card to delete a log.

task: Generate a full course with AI (AI Course Creator)
content_text: Describe a course and let AI build a complete outline with slides. 1. Open 'AI Course Creator' from the AI section of the sidebar (or 'Create course from AI' under Course Creation). 2. In the course goal box, describe the course, or click one of the example prompts to prefill it. 3. Set the Course Settings pills: Skill Level, AI Model (default 'Auto (Smart)'), number of chapters, duration, and slides per chapter. 4. Open 'Content Options' to include items like Diagrams, Code Snippets, Quizzes, Assignments, YouTube Videos, AI Videos, or AI Slides. 5. Optionally add 'References' (URLs or PDF/DOC/CSV/XLSX files) for the AI to use as context. 6. Click 'Generate Course Outline', review the configuration in the 'Review Course Details' dialog, then click 'Continue' to start generation.

task: Review the AI course outline and create the course
content_text: After generation, edit the AI outline and turn it into a real course. 1. On the 'Step 1: Review Your Course Outline' screen, expand chapters to see their pages; use 'Expand All' / 'Collapse All'. 2. Edit titles, drag to reorder, and use the per-item controls to regenerate, view, add, or delete chapters and pages. 3. Click 'Generate Page Content' to have AI fill in the slide content for the outline. 4. When content is ready, click 'Create Course' (admins) or 'Create Draft Course' (teachers) to add it to the library. 5. You can leave and resume later — a saved draft appears as a 'Resume' banner back on the AI Course Creator screen.

task: Add your own AI API keys and handle AI credits
content_text: AI generation can run on your own provider keys, and credit limits surface as errors. 1. In the AI Course Creator, click the 'API Keys' pill (it turns green once a key is added). 2. Paste an 'OpenRouter API Key' and/or a 'Gemini API Key' and click the add (+) button to save; a green 'Added' tag confirms it. 3. Use the linked openrouter.ai or Google AI Studio pages if you need to create a key. 4. Remove a saved key with the trash icon. 5. If a generation fails with a credits message, your OpenRouter credits are exhausted — recharge them (or add a key with available credit) and retry.

task: Export learner data to a CSV file
content_text: Download the learner list (with the columns you choose) as a CSV that respects your current filters. 1. Open the learner list from the sidebar (Manage Contacts then the linked-course contacts list, at /manage-students/students-list). 2. Optionally set the Session selector and apply any search or filter chips so only the learners you want are included. 3. Click the 'Export Data' button at the top right. 4. In the 'Export Data' dialog, tick the columns to include (use 'Select all' / 'Clear all'; columns are grouped by category). 5. Click 'Export'. 6. Wait for 'Preparing your export...' to finish; the file downloads as students_export_<date>.csv and a toast confirms how many records were exported.

task: Export learner account login details
content_text: Download a CSV of learner account/login details for the learners matching your current filters. 1. Open the learner list from the sidebar (Manage Contacts, then the linked-course contacts list at /manage-students/students-list). 2. Choose the Session and apply any search or filter chips to narrow the learners. 3. Click the 'Export account details' button at the top right (next to 'Export Data'). 4. The button shows 'Exporting...' while it works, then downloads the file as students_export_<date>.csv. Note: this is the account-details export and is separate from the column-pickable 'Export Data' option.

task: Generate and download certificates for learners
content_text: Create certificates for selected learners from your own template and download them. 1. Open the learner list at /manage-students/students-list and tick the learners you want certificates for. 2. From the bulk actions menu, choose 'Create Certificate' to open Certificate Generation with those learners. 3. On the 'Student Data & CSV Upload' step, review the selected learners (optionally upload a CSV to add extra dynamic fields), then continue to 'PDF Annotation'. 4. In the 'Upload' view, upload your certificate template (PDF or image). 5. Switch to 'Design' and drag fields (e.g. Student Name, Enrollment Number, Completion Date) from the palette onto the template. 6. Use 'Preview' to check how certificates look with real learner data. 7. Click 'Generate Certificates'; progress is shown and the certificates download when complete.

task: View the AI credit usage report and export it
content_text: See how many AI credits each team member used and export the detail to Excel. 1. Open Settings from the sidebar and select the 'AI Settings' tab. 2. In the left section nav, click 'Usage' (the 'AI Credit Usage by User' section). 3. Use the date-range filter (defaults to last 7 days), the role sub-tabs (All plus a tab per role), and the 'Search by name or email' box to scope the data. 4. Each row shows Name, Email, Role, Credits used and Requests; click 'View logs' on a row to open that member's conversations and credit activity. 5. Click 'Export to Excel' to download a workbook with Summary, Activity Log, Chat Sessions and Chat Messages sheets for the currently filtered range.

task: Get started as a Content Creator (your dashboard)
content_text: As a Content Creator your dashboard is course-authoring focused (no finance or institute KPIs). 1. Land on the Dashboard from the sidebar after signing in. 2. Use the Quick Actions bar at the top: 'My Courses' opens your course list and 'New Course' starts a new one (both go to the Courses page). 3. The 'My Courses' widget gives quick access to your authored courses and submissions; click 'View All' to open the full list. 4. 'Pending Actions' shows work that needs your attention. 5. The AI Features card at the bottom highlights AI authoring tools and can be dismissed.

task: Find the courses you authored (Content Creator)
content_text: The Courses page opens on your own work first. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar (page heading 'Explore Courses'). 2. The 'Authored Courses' tab is selected by default and lists the courses you created. 3. Switch to 'All Courses' to browse every course, or 'Courses In Review' to track ones you submitted for approval. 4. Use the search box to find a specific course by name. 5. Click a course card to open its details and continue authoring.

task: Track courses you submitted for review (Content Creator)
content_text: After you submit a course for approval it moves out of your hands until an admin reviews it. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar. 2. Click the 'Courses In Review' tab (heading 'Courses In Review'). 3. Each submitted course shows an 'In Review' badge so you can see what is pending approval. 4. From here you can track status and withdraw a course from review if you need to keep editing it. Admins approve or reject submissions from the separate 'Course Approval' tab.

task: Author course content with the AI Copilot (Content Creator)
content_text: Content Creators can generate a course outline and slides instead of building everything by hand. 1. Open the Courses section from the sidebar. 2. On the Explore Courses page, click 'Create Course with AI' (shown when enabled for your role). 3. You are taken to the AI Copilot, where you describe the course so it can draft the outline and content. 4. Review and refine the generated structure, then continue editing the course normally. Note: the 'Create Course with AI' button only appears when it is enabled in your role's display settings.

task: Get started as an Assessment Creator (your dashboard)
content_text: As an Assessment Creator your dashboard is assessment-focused (no finance or institute KPIs). 1. Land on the Dashboard from the sidebar after signing in. 2. Use the Quick Actions bar: 'Assessments' opens the Assessments area and 'Reports' opens learning reports. 3. The Assessment Center widget summarizes your assessment work. 4. 'Pending Actions' lists items that need your attention. 5. The AI Features card at the bottom highlights AI question/assessment tools and can be dismissed.

task: Start a new assessment by type (Assessment Creator)
content_text: The Assessments page is where you pick the kind of test to build. 1. Open Assessments from the sidebar (heading 'Create Assessment'). 2. Choose a type by clicking its card: 'Examination' (fixed-time, scheduled), 'Mock Assessment' (always-available timed practice), 'Practice Assessment' (on-demand, no time limit), 'Survey' (feedback questions, no right answer), or 'Manual Upload Exam' (students solve offline and upload answer sheets). 3. Selecting a type opens the multi-step assessment builder so you can fill in details and add questions. 4. Use the builder steps to finish and publish the assessment.

task: Manage your question bank and papers (Assessment Creator)
content_text: Build a reusable library of question papers separate from any single assessment. 1. Open the Question Papers page from the sidebar (heading 'Question Paper Access & Management'). 2. Click 'Add Question Paper'. 3. Choose 'Create Manually' to type questions in, or 'Upload from Device' to import a file. 4. Fill in the title and subject/tag details for the paper. 5. Save it so the paper is available to reuse when you build assessments later.

task: Track the assessments you created by status (Assessment Creator)
content_text: Scheduled Tests lists all the assessments you created, grouped by status. 1. Open Scheduled Tests from the Assessments group in the sidebar. 2. Switch between the Live, Upcoming, Previous, and Drafts tabs to find a test. 3. Use the search and filters to narrow the list. 4. Open the '...' menu on a test and choose View Assessment Details to inspect it, or Delete Assessment to remove it. Use Drafts to return to assessments you started but have not published yet.
